sources.title: Cambridge International A Level Further Mathematics 9231 syllabus

statements.statementText: recall and use the relations between the roots and coefficients of polynomial equations
notesText: e.g. to evaluate symmetric functions of the roots or to solve problems involving unknown coefficients in equations; restricted to equations of degree 2, 3 or 4 only.

reviewNotes: Directly mentions symmetric function evaluation.

statements.statementText: use a substitution to obtain an equation whose roots are related in a simple way to those of the original equation
notesText: Substitutions will not be given for the easiest cases, e.g. where the new roots are reciprocals or squares or a simple linear function of the old roots.

statements.statementText: understand and use relationships between the graphs of y = f(x), y² = f(x), y = 1/f(x), y = |f(x)| and y = f(|x|).
notesText: Including use of such sketch graphs in the course of solving equations or inequalities.

conceptLinks.evidenceSpan: use of such sketch graphs in the course of solving equations or inequalities
reviewNotes: The node covers solving equations graphically via intersection points; inequalities are a natural extension but not explicitly mentioned in the node definition.

statements.statementText: sketch graphs of simple rational functions, including the determination of oblique asymptotes, in cases where the degree of the numerator and the denominator are at most 2
notesText: Including determination of the set of values taken by the function, e.g. by the use of a discriminant. Detailed plotting of curves will not be required, but sketches will generally be expected to show significant features, such as turning points, asymptotes and intersections with the axes.

reviewNotes: Reciprocal functions are a common subset of simple rational functions; node covers y=1/f(x) and related forms.

conceptLinks.evidenceSpan: determination of the set of values taken by the function
reviewNotes: Statement explicitly requires finding the set of values (range) of a function, which is a narrower aspect of the domain/range node.

conceptLinks.evidenceSpan: intersections with the axes
reviewNotes: The statement requires sketches to show intersections with axes, which involves locating intersection points of the curve with the coordinate axes, a specific application of curve intersection concepts.

conceptLinks.evidenceSpan: Including determination of the set of values taken by the function, e.g. by the use of a discriminant. Detailed plotting of curves will not be required, but sketches will generally be expected to show significant features, such as turning points, asymptotes and intersections with the axes.
reviewNotes: The notes explicitly require sketches to show turning points, which are local maxima or minima. The node CALC-DIFF-APPL-MAXM defines exactly this concept and its classification using derivative tests, matching the required skill.
